title: Lec-47: Disadvantages of Classful Addressing | IP addressing | Computer Networks

segments.text: Hello friends, welcome to Gate Smashers. The topic is Problems with Classful IP addressing.
In Classful IP addressing the first and very important problem is wastage of IP addresses.
Why there is a wastage of IP addresses? In Class A, we have already discussed in Class A
that number of hosts in each network is 2 raised to 24.
Which is a very huge number, more than 1 crore.
The number is very huge. Now here if I have, even if we talk about the large MNCs,
even the large Multinational Companies don't have requirement of these many hosts.
And if I talk about Class B, in Class B there are 2 raised to 16
2 raised to 16 number of hosts in every network which is about approximately 65000.
Now if we see here, 65000 is a very huge value, although it is moderate.
Means there are lot of organisations which can use this, but this is very huge.
And here if I talk about network in Class A, there are very limited networks of Class A
because there are only 126 networks possible in Class A.
But in each network more than 1 crore number of hosts can be possible.
If we talk about Class C, number of hosts in every network can be 256
which is a very low number. Means here I have problem of flexibility first.
What is concept of flexibility? If any organisation or person comes to me and says
I need let's say 1024 number of IP addresses.
Now to provide this many IP addresses what solution do I have?
Either I give Class C address, Class C is insufficient
because Class C has very less number of IP addreses.
And if I talk about Class A, if I give him Class A or Class B addresses,
then he needs only a thousand, but if I give Class B
then the remaining 64000 will be wasted.
And if I give Class A IP address then a huge number of IP addresses is wasted.
So here I have wastage of IP addresses,
along with that there is a problem with the flexibility also.
Means according to user demand we are not providing IP addresses,
already we have manually cut beforehand that
this has this many IP addresses, this many in this, this many in this class.
If a user comes to me, according to that I am fulfiling his demand.
But because of that many of my IP addresses can be wasted.
Already if I talk about Class D and CLass E, then in Class D and CLass E,
in Class D 2 raised to 28,
2 raised to 28 number of IP addresses are wasted because of multicasting.
And in Class E also 2 raised to 28 number of hosts are reserved for future purpose.
Means nearly 500 crore numbers are already reserved or wasted here.
So that's why the biggest problem in Classful Addressing is wastage of IP addresses.
Now, the second point is maintenance is very time consuming.
Because if I talk about Class A, then if I have a large network in Class A,
then in such a big network there will be a problem with the maintenance.
So maintenance will be where? It is possible in small networks.
To remove or reduce this maintenance cost we can use funda of subnetting here.
What does subnetting do? It divides large network into small networks.
So maintaining small networks is easy compared to big network.
More prone to errors. Again the same problem that if network is so big,
if problem occurs anywhere in that network, some hardware or software failure problem,
it is very difficult to track first. And after tracking,
repairing the problem will be very time consuming.
And in big network obviously there will be more chances of errors.
Other than this there will be problem with the security also.
Means as big the network is there I will have authorization and authentication problems
because unauthorized users will try to access the system.
Means if I put simply, if I have a big house then many vulnerablilities or loopholes are made,
that a thief can enter the house. But smaller the network I have smaller the chances.
But I am not saying take a small network. If some organization demands a big network
then take a big network. But we can convert that big network logically into subnets
from which I get this advantage, that maintenance is also not time consuming,
errors also reduce, security is also provided more.
But here in classful security problem comes. And if I talk about wastage of IP addresses,
for this also I can use funda of subnetting, or classless addressing.
That is the best point that if user says that he wants only 1024 networks, 1024 IP addresses.
Then classless says that you will get only 1024. Neither more nor less.
Means that will exactly give the values whatever the user demands.
So that's why you can use classless because
that is a very flexible method of poviding IP addresses.
So these are the major problems or drawbacks that occur in Classful Addressing.
Thank you.
